title: Uav-supported mobile communications network
abstract: A facility operates with respect to a configuration of one or more unmanned aerial vehicles operating as relays between on or more wireless network participant devices and one or more wireless base stations being supported directly or indirectly by a planetary surface. The facility conducts an experiment that yields quality of service results and flight duration results for each of multiple values of a UAV control parameter. The facility selects a value of the UAV control parameter that produced an advantageous tradeoff of quality of service results and flight duration results, and stores the selected value, such that the stored value of the UAV control parameter is usable to operate the UAVs of the configuration in production service.
claims: Claims 1 . A wireless network, comprising: one or more stationary radio base stations, each base station being supported directly or indirectly by a planetary surface, and comprising: one or more radios configured to transmit and receive data wirelessly, and a connection to a terrestrial network configured to convey data to other points supported directly or indirectly by the planetary surface; and one or more unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs), each UAV comprising: flight systems configured to induce flight of the UAV through a series of three-dimensional positions each characterized by an altitude and a lateral location, one or more radios configured to (1 ) transmit and receive data wirelessly with radios of at least one network participant device, and (2) transmit and receive data wirelessly with at least one radio of at least one base station, a router configured to use the radios to relay data wirelessly between the network participant devices and the base stations, an energy storage system configured to provide energy to the one or more radios, a memory storing values for each of at least one UAV control parameter, the stored control parameter values selected to produce a desirable tradeoff between a quality of service level that can be achieved for network participant devices and a an amount of time for which the UAV can remain in service, and a control system configured to control the UAV in accordance with the control parameters stored by the memory. 2. The wireless network of claim 1 , further comprising: one or more network participant devices, each comprising one or more radios configured transmit and receive data wirelessly with radios of at least one UAV. 3. The wireless network of claim 2 wherein a selected one of the network participant devices further comprises a battery configured to store energy for powering the one or more radios of the selected network participant device. 4. The wireless network of claim 2 wherein a selected one of the network participant devices further comprises a connection to utility power configured to convey energy for powering the one or more radios of the selected network participant device. 5. The wireless network of claim 1 wherein the UAV control parameters for which the memory stores values comprise a flight elevation control parameter. 6. The wireless network of claim 5 wherein the flight elevation control parameter value stored by the memory is approximately 140m. 7. The wireless network of claim 5 wherein the flight elevation control parameter value stored by the memory is between 130m and 150m. 8. The wireless network of claim 1 wherein the UAV control parameters for which the memory stores values comprise a flight lateral path shape control parameter. 9. The wireless network of claim 1 wherein the UAV control parameters for which the memory stores values comprise a radio beamwidth angle control parameter. 10. The wireless network of claim 1 wherein the UAV control parameters for which the memory stores values comprise a UAV cell area control parameter or a UAV cell radius control parameter. 11 . The wireless network of claim 1 wherein the UAV control parameters for which the memory stores values comprise a broadcast power level control parameter. 12. An unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV), comprising: flight systems configured to induce flight of the UAV through a series of three-dimensional positions each characterized by an altitude and a lateral location; one or more radios configured to (1) transmit and receive data wirelessly with radios of at least one network participant device, and (2) transmit and receive data wirelessly with at least one radio of at least one base station supported directly or indirectly by a planetary surface; a router configured to use the radios to relay data wirelessly between the network participant devices and the base stations; an energy storage system configured to provide energy to the one or more radios; a memory storing values for each of at least one UAV control parameter, the stored control parameter values selected to produce a desirable tradeoff between a quality of service level that can be achieved for network participant devices and a an amount of time for which the UAV can remain in service; and a control system configured to control the UAV in accordance with the control parameters stored by the memory. 13. The UAV of claim 12 wherein the memory stores a value of a flight elevation control parameter value approximately equal to 140m. 14. The UAV of claim 12 wherein the memory stores a value of a radio beamwidth angle control parameter. 15. The UAV of claim 12 wherein the memory stores a value of a UAV cell area control parameter or a UAV cell radius control parameter. 16. The UAV of claim 12 the memory stores a value of a broadcast power level control parameter. 17. A method with respect to a configuration of one or more unmanned aerial vehicles operating as relays between on or more wireless network participant devices and one or more wireless base stations being supported directly or indirectly by a planetary surface, comprising: conducting an experiment that yields quality of service results and flight duration results for each of multiple values of a UAV control parameter; selecting a value of the UAV control parameter that produced an advantageous tradeoff of quality of service results and flight duration results; and storing the selected value, such that the stored value of the UAV control parameter is usable to operate the UAVs of the configuration in production service. 18. The method of claim 17 wherein conducting the experiment comprises conducting a simulation experiment. 19. The method of claim 17 wherein the conducting the experiment comprises conducting a practical experiment. 20. The method of claim 17, further comprising: using the stored value of the UAV control parameter to operate the UAVs of the configuration in production service.
